results.title: I grew to 100,000+ Instagram followers in 3 years But on LinkedIn? You'll grow even more quickly. LinkedIn has 1.1+ Billion active users. But under 5% share content weekly. Just one post per day… | Lauren Tickner | 80 comments
results.content:
"I'm not getting any clients from LinkedIn." I hear this complaint constantly and here's what's really happening: 1. You're focusing on follower count instead of real connections 2. You're pitching strangers who don't know you exist 3. You're treating LinkedIn like a billboard Most people think LinkedIn success means posting once and waiting for leads to flood in. That's not how it works. Here's what actually drives results: → Comment meaningfully on your prospects' posts daily → Share valuable insights without asking for anything in return → Send personalized messages that start conversations, not sales pitches The magic happens in the comments section and DMs not your feed 📱 Your ideal clients are already active on LinkedIn right now.   Before: When I first started on LinkedIn, I was lost. No clear niche. No real direction. I posted anything that came to mind. And every post felt like shouting into an empty room. Low views. Few likes. Zero leads. Confidence? Almost none. After: Then I decided to understand LinkedIn. Clear on my niche. Clear on who I wanted to serve. Clear on the value I bring. Once the vision sharpened, everything changed. Posts started getting traction. Views and likes went up. Inbound leads started coming in. Outbound replies doubled. Opportunities I never imagined landed in my inbox. If you feel invisible on LinkedIn right now, It’s not about working harder. It’s about getting clear on your niche, your voice, and your value.

results.title: Proven LinkedIn Growth Strategies
results.content:
View profile for ASHITA JAIN

Helping B2B founders go from invisible to 25+ inbound leads/month on LinkedIn | Instagram Growth | Co-Founder @LEADNEURALS [...] My LinkedIn grew by 50K in 365 days.
Here are 7 tips to help grow yours:
1. Stop posting about everything
Pick 2-3 topics you actually care about.
Not what’s trending.
Not what others are doing.
Choose what you want to be known for.
Own those topics. Repeat them.
Build a reputation around consistency.
2. Give more than you take
Share value without expecting anything in return.
No pitch. No agenda. Just help.
Trust me ↓
It always comes back.
Often in unexpected ways.
Usually when you need it most.
3. Forget perfection
Instead: Start sharing your story.
(this is where most of my clients get stuck)
People don’t connect with polish. They connect with real.
Your journey is your most powerful content.
4. Don’t ignore the comments
That’s where real connections start. [...] I grew from 0 to 200,000 followers on LinkedIn with zero ads, just strategy.
Here’s EXACTLY what worked (and what didn’t):
No fluff.
Just raw, real, straight-up DOs & DON’Ts that got me here.
✅ Post daily (or at least 4x a week) – Consistency beats perfection.
✅ Master the hook – If your first line is weak, nobody reads the rest.
✅ Tell stories – People remember stories, not facts.
✅ Use visuals – Text-only is great, but visuals get insane engagement.
✅ Engage like crazy – 30 min before & after posting, comment on relevant posts.
✅ Leverage LinkedIn DMs – Your network isn’t just in comments, it’s in your inbox.
✅ Have a unique tone – Bold? Witty? Data-driven? Find your edge & own it.
✅ Make people feel something – Emotion = virality.

Growing on LinkedIn is not about posting every day or chasing trends. It’s about being intentional.  
  
Small improvements, made consistently, often deliver better results than occasional bursts of activity.  
  
We’ve put together 10 practical LinkedIn marketing hacks that can help you improve your visibility, build credibility, and grow your professional network over time.  
  
Whether you’re a founder, job seeker, freelancer, or business professional, there’s something here you can apply today.  
  
Swipe through the carousel, save it for future reference, and share it with someone who’s serious about growing on LinkedIn.
